description: Rules for ensuring the application handles errors gracefully at every layer (UI, API, Forms) without crashing.
philosophy: Errors are not exceptions; they are a predictable part of the user flow and must be handled elegantly.

rules.name: Global UI Error Boundary
rules.description: The entire application or its main layout MUST be wrapped in a global React Error Boundary component.
rules.purpose: To catch any unexpected rendering errors in the component tree and display a user-friendly fallback UI instead of a blank screen.
rules.implementation: Create a `components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx` and use it in the root `layout.tsx`.

rules.name: API Error Handling Mandate
rules.description: All data-fetching hooks (using React Query) MUST handle the `isError` state.
guidelines: If `isError` is true, the component must render a specific error message or a fallback component.
Do not let API errors bubble up and crash the UI. The hook should return a structured error object.
Display user-friendly messages (e.g., 'Failed to load data') instead of technical error details.

rules.name: Form Validation & Feedback
rules.description: All user input forms MUST provide clear, field-level validation feedback.
rules.implementation: Use Zod for schema validation and a library like `react-hook-form` to display error messages directly under the invalid input fields.
rules.prohibited: Relying on a single `alert()` for form errors is forbidden.
